author: Cosmakes
description: This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff. Hierarchical visual database for mouse neuroscience data, built on the Allen Mouse Brain Atlas (CCFv3).

manifest.name: MapTheMind

name: MapTheMind

releases.author: Cosmakes
releases.description: The name of the plugin was changed to adhere to Obsidian naming guidelines.
releases.name: 1.1.1 Changed Plugin Name to adhere to Obsidian naming guideline

releases.author: Cosmakes
releases.description:
##  New

- **Logical AND in search** — type `CA3 AND mossy fiber` to require both phrases in a note.
- **Customizable schematic cells** — when adding a cell without a morphology, the placement panel now offers a soma shape (circle / triangle / oval) plus four shape sliders: primary dendrites, angular spread, branch depth, and arborization strength.
- **Drag to rotate the 3D brain** — drag the canvas to spin the camera (default on). Adjustable in the plugin settings.

## Improvements

- 3D search preview cards now stay put while you rotate and ease smoothly into any new position.
- The scale slider now resizes schematic cells (previously this did not work).
- Refreshed help (`?` button in every view) to cover the new features.

##  Fixes

- The viewpoint search-results panel no longer covers the rotate / reset / help buttons.
- Typing the letter `r` in the cell-name input field now inserts the letter instead of rotating the placement preview.
releases.name: MapTheMind 1.1.0 - Bug Fixes, 3D view drag navigation and new search functionality

releases.author: Cosmakes
releases.description:
First public release. Neuro Mindmap (Mouse) turns your Obsidian vault into a visual neuroscience database anchored to the Allen Mouse Brain Atlas (CCFv3): browse the whole brain in 3D, drill into reference sections, and attach markdown notes to any region, viewpoint, or cell.

Desktop only. Cached atlas data can grow to ~200 MB after extensive use; safe to delete at any time.
releases.name: 1.0.0 — initial release
